Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "Mosaic" or similar) to find company_mosaic, active deals including Zara Okoye relocation (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026), Deshawn Hartley (deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026), Keiko Tanaka (deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026), and the damage risk deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk).
2. List CRM engagements for Mosaic using crm_list_engagements (company_id: company_mosaic or similar) to find the damage incident engagement documenting $90,000 prototype value, $50,000 vendor liability cap, Blessing Okafor remote sign-off from Philadelphia, Heartland Movers work order, and Tamara Byrd escalation.
3. Search QuickBooks invoices for Mosaic using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Mosaic" or similar) to find INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 for Okoye + Hartley + Tanaka relocations with surcharges pending client approval) and CM-2026-0415 (negative $90,000 credit memo for prototype damage).
4. Search QuickBooks bills or accounts using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_accounts (query: "Mosaic" or "accrual" or similar) to find the $90,000 contingent liability accrual (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC) and the $89 Andre Washington stipend hold (STIP-2026-0094).
5. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "Tamara" or "damage" or similar) to find Tamara Byrd's escalation email demanding an incident report within 48 hours and related damage correspondence.
6. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or "coverage gap" or similar) to find Blessing Okafor's admission about remote sign-off in #operations, Marcus Thorne's insurance gap analysis in #finance ($50,000 cap, $40,000 exposure), and Hana Kim's accounting entries (credit memo and accrual).
7. Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (query: "Mosaic" or similar) to find all three Mosaic relocations: Zara Okoye Savannah to Atlanta (Completed), Deshawn Hartley Tampa to Denver (In Progress, hard deadline April 19 for Automate 2026 trade show), Keiko Tanaka Columbus to Seattle (In Progress, laser cutter requiring DOT hazmat placarding).
8. Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or similar) to find DOT compliance audit issues OPS-1 through OPS-5 including OPS-2 Tanaka (in_progress, P1, missing placarding certificate). This links Keiko Tanaka's Mosaic move to the active PHMSA compliance inquiry.
9. Search Airtable for stipend records using airtable_search_records (query: "stipend" or "Andre Washington" or similar) and search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "stipend" or "duplicate" or similar) to find Andre Washington's $89 duplicate receipt (TechComfort Supply, admin hold) and Kenji Nakamura's coworking stipend error.
10. Search CRM for GreenStack Energy using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "GreenStack" or similar) to find the company record, active deals for Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes April relocations, and the GreenStack engagement history.
11. Search QuickBooks for GreenStack using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "GreenStack" or similar) to find INV-2026-0418 ($9,440 including hazmat surcharge $1,800, interim storage $1,650 pending Derek approval, rig surcharge $950, April stipend $2,000).
12. List CRM engagements for GreenStack using crm_list_engagements to find the April wave engagement detailing Swift transport, Pacific Crest storage arrangement, and whether Derek Hollis approved the $1,650 storage line.
13. Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (query: "Seattle" or "Fitzgerald" or similar) to find Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack, PHL to SEA, move-in April 28, 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B). Search emails using search_emails (query: "Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom Seattle" or similar) or Slack using conversations_search_messages to find Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom, PHL to SEA, move-in April 14, same unit 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B). Noah Fitzpatrick does not have an Airtable relocation record. Comparing both records confirms the housing double-booking at the same apartment.
14. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "PHMSA" or "DOT" or "compliance" or similar) to find Marcus Thorne's disclosure of the formal DOT inquiry with penalties up to $59,017 per violation per day, the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual, and the extended documentation deadline of June 5, 2026. This connects DOT compliance to both Mosaic (Tanaka laser) and GreenStack (Zhao lithium batteries).
15. Look up contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or "Tamara" or similar) to get david.chen@moveops.com and tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com for the emails.
16. Search calendar using calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events to check whether Emeka has a Mosaic check-in or Tamara call this week. Expected discovery: no existing meeting.
17. Send email using send_email to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com acknowledging the delay on the incident report and proposing next steps to rebuild the relationship.
18. Send email using send_email to david.chen@moveops.com with a full status report covering Mosaic financials ($90K damage, $50K cap, $40K gap, $9,010 invoice, credit memo), GreenStack April invoice status ($1,650 storage pending), the Seattle apartment double-booking (Fitzpatrick vs Fitzgerald), DOT compliance risks across both accounts, and Andre Washington stipend hold.
19. Post message using conversations_add_message to the CE team channel with a high-level heads-up that Mosaic and GreenStack need attention. Exclude employee stipend or HR details per prompt constraint.
20. Update CRM using crm_create_engagement on company_mosaic with notes on the current account status, Tamara escalation, and damage situation.
21. Update CRM using crm_create_engagement on the GreenStack account with notes on the April invoice status and any outstanding items.
22. Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the Tamara incident report response, since no existing ticket covers this obligation.
23. Add a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for a Mosaic check-in or Tamara call this week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

4. Search QuickBooks bills or accounts using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_accounts (query: "Mosaic" or "accrual" or similar) to find the $90,000 contingent liability accrual (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC) and the $89 Andre Washington stipend hold (STIP-2026-0094).

Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or "coverage gap" or similar) to find Blessing Okafor's admission about remote sign-off in #operations, Marcus Thorne's insurance gap analysis in #finance ($50,000 cap, $40,000 exposure), and Hana Kim's accounting entries (credit memo and accrual).
7. 8. Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or similar) to find DOT compliance audit issues OPS-1 through OPS-5 including OPS-2 Tanaka (in_progress, P1, missing placarding certificate). This links Keiko Tanaka's Mosaic move to the active PHMSA compliance inquiry.
9. Search Airtable for stipend records using airtable_search_records (query: "stipend" or "Andre Washington" or similar) and search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "stipend" or "duplicate" or similar) to find Andre Washington's $89 duplicate receipt (TechComfort Supply, admin hold) and Kenji Nakamura's coworking stipend error.
10. Search CRM for GreenStack Energy using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "GreenStack" or similar) to find the company record, active deals for Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes April relocations, and the GreenStack engagement history.
11. Search QuickBooks for GreenStack using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "GreenStack" or similar) to find INV-2026-0418 ($9,440 including hazmat surcharge $1,800, interim storage $1,650 pending Derek approval, rig surcharge $950, April stipend $2,000).
12. List CRM engagements for GreenStack using crm_list_engagements to find the April wave engagement detailing Swift transport, Pacific Crest storage arrangement, and whether Derek Hollis approved the $1,650 storage line.
13. Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (query: "Seattle" or "Fitzgerald" or similar) to find Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack, PHL to SEA, move-in April 28, 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B). Search emails using search_emails (query: "Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom Seattle" or similar) or Slack using conversations_search_messages to find Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom, PHL to SEA, move-in April 14, same unit 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B). Noah Fitzpatrick does not have an Airtable relocation record. Comparing both records confirms the housing double-booking at the same apartment.
14. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "PHMSA" or "DOT" or "compliance" or similar) to find Marcus Thorne's disclosure of the formal DOT inquiry with penalties up to $59,017 per violation per day, the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual, and the extended documentation deadline of June 5, 2026. This connects DOT compliance to both Mosaic (Tanaka laser) and GreenStack (Zhao lithium batteries).
15. Look up contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or "Tamara" or similar) to get david.chen@moveops.com and tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com for the emails.
16. Search calendar using calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events to check whether Emeka has a Mosaic check-in or Tamara call this week. Expected discovery: no existing meeting.
17. Send email using send_email to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com acknowledging the delay on the incident report and proposing next steps to rebuild the relationship.
18. Send email using send_email to david.chen@moveops.com with a full status report covering Mosaic financials ($90K damage, $50K cap, $40K gap, $9,010 invoice, credit memo), GreenStack April invoice status ($1,650 storage pending), the Seattle apartment double-booking (Fitzpatrick vs Fitzgerald), DOT compliance risks across both accounts, and Andre Washington stipend hold.
19. Post message using conversations_add_message to the CE team channel with a high-level heads-up that Mosaic and GreenStack need attention. Exclude employee stipend or HR details per prompt constraint.
20. Update CRM using crm_create_engagement on company_mosaic with notes on the current account status, Tamara escalation, and damage situation.
21. Update CRM using crm_create_engagement on the GreenStack account with notes on the April invoice status and any outstanding items.
22. Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the Tamara incident report response, since no existing ticket covers this obligation.
23. Add a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for a Mosaic check-in or Tamara call this week.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a CRM read tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_list_engagements) to retrieve account data.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used multiple CRM read tools: crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom), crm_search_contacts (for Tamara, Derek, Zara Okoye), crm_search_deals (for all three companies), and crm_list_engagements (for Mosaic and GreenStack contacts). These calls retrieved real account data including engagement history showing the damage incident timeline and open commitments.
✓Run #2The agent used crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom), crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals (for all three companies), and crm_list_engagements (for contact_mosaic_hr and contact_greenstack_hr), all returning data that informed the final report.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called multiple CRM read tools: crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom), crm_search_contacts (for Tamara, Derek, David), crm_search_deals (for all three companies), and crm_list_engagements (for Mosaic and GreenStack contacts). These calls returned rich account and engagement data used in the report.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom), crm_search_contacts (for Tamara, Derek, Zara Okoye), crm_search_deals (for all three companies), and crm_list_engagements (for company_mosaic and company_greenstack). Multiple CRM read tools were used to retrieve account data.
✓Run #6The agent used crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom), crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals, and crm_list_engagements — all CRM read tools — to retrieve account data before composing the report.
2 The model must use a QuickBooks read tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_search_accounts) to retrieve financial records.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used quickbooks_search_invoices (multiple times, including with specific customer refs and fetchAll), and quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll). The all-invoices call returned detailed invoice data for Mosaic and GreenStack which the agent used to populate the financial section of the David report with actual invoice numbers, amounts, and due dates.
✓Run #2The agent used quickbooks_search_invoices to retrieve INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0418, INV-2026-0306, and also quickbooks_search_customers. The invoice data directly populated the financial figures in the David email and Tamara email.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (multiple times with different criteria and a fetchAll call), quickbooks_search_customers (multiple times), quickbooks_read_invoice (for INV-2026-0411 and INV-2026-0418), and quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll). The initial specific queries returned no results but the broader fetchAll call returned a large dataset (68,854 chars saved to file).
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices with empty criteria {} to retrieve all invoices, then used a Bash command to extract Mosaic and GreenStack invoices from the results. The large QB file contained invoice data used in the David email (INV-2026-0411 for $9,010, CM-2026-0415 credit memo, ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC).
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices for INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic) and INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack), retrieving actual financial records from QuickBooks rather than relying on Slack estimates.
3 The model must use an Airtable read tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to retrieve relocation records.
tool selection
 ———✓✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
—Run #2No justification
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times: by Company for Mosaic, GreenStack, and Axiom; by Destination for 'Seattle'; and by Client for Mosaic, GreenStack, and Axiom in the Client Accounts table. The agent also called airtable_list_tables and airtable_list_bases to discover the schema.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times: for Mosaic Relocations, GreenStack Relocations, Seattle Destination, Axiom Relocations, Client Accounts for both Mosaic and GreenStack, and Stipend Transactions. Airtable read tools were clearly used.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_search_records on 'Relocations' table for Mosaic, GreenStack, Axiom, and Seattle-destination records. It successfully retrieved relocation data including move statuses, coordinator assignments, and special requirements.
4 The model must use a Linear read tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to retrieve compliance or ticket data.
tool selection
 ✓✗—✓✗✗ 40%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'Mosaic' and retrieved results including the DOT compliance issue (OPS-2, issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) about the missing placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's Class 3B laser cutter. This was surfaced in the David email.
✗Run #2No Linear tool calls appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent gathered DOT compliance information from Slack message searches instead of using a Linear read tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue). The Slack messages referenced Linear ticket OPS-2, but the agent never queried Linear directly.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called linear_get_issue with id 'OPS-2' (which returned 'issue_not_found') and called linear_list_users. While the direct ticket lookup failed, the agent still used a Linear read tool as required by the criterion.
✗Run #5No Linear tool calls appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent searched Slack for 'DOT compliance' but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to retrieve the DOT compliance tickets (OPS-1 through OPS-5). DOT information in the final report appears to have come from Slack/email searches, not Linear directly.
✗Run #6No Linear tool call (linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent found DOT compliance info via Slack messages, not by querying Linear directly. The Linear ticket OPS-2 was referenced in Slack data but the agent never queried Linear.
5 The model must use a calendar read tool (e.g., calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events) to check Emeka's schedule before adding a calendar event.
tool selection
 ✗✗—✗✓✗ 20%
✗Run #1No calendar read tool call (calendar_search_events, calendar_read_today_calendar_events, or equivalent) appears anywhere in the trajectory before the calendar_add_calendar_event call. The agent directly created the event without first checking whether an existing Mosaic or Tamara meeting was already on the calendar.
✗Run #2The agent went directly to calendar_add_calendar_event without first calling any calendar read/search tool (e.g., calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events) to check whether a Mosaic or Tamara meeting already existed. No calendar read tool call is present in the trajectory.
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4The agent called calendar_add_calendar_event without first calling any calendar read tool (e.g., calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events). No calendar read tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory before the event was created.
✓Run #5The agent called both calendar_read_today_calendar_events and calendar_search_events (querying for 'Mosaic') before creating the calendar event. Both returned no existing events, confirming no duplicate check-in existed.
✗Run #6The agent never called a calendar read tool (e.g., calendar_search_events or calendar_read_today_calendar_events) before creating the Mosaic check-in event. It went directly to calendar_add_calendar_event without checking for pre-existing meetings.
6 An email was sent (via send_email) to [david.chen@moveops.com](mailto:david.chen@moveops.com) and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called send_email with recipients: ['david.chen@moveops.com'] and the tool returned success with email_id 'f8d1aa94b3fb44dfa0f0fdf683cafbaf'. The email was a comprehensive cross-account risk report.
